data.title: SmartCentres to distribute $0.15417 per unit for January 2026, $1.85 annually. Payable on Feb 17, 2026, to shareholders.
data.description: In Toronto, SmartCentres Real Estate Investment Trust (TSX: SRU.UN) has reported a distribution for January 2026, with trustees declaring a payment of $0.15417 per unit. This equates to an annualized amount of $1.85 per unit. The distribution is set to be paid on February 17, 2026, to unitholders recorded as of January 30, 2026. SmartCentres is recognized as one of Canada's prominent fully integrated REITs, displaying continuous growth and top-tier operations.

data.title: Sei Network discontinues Cosmos support for 200K TPS goal. Migrate from CosmWasm to EVM by mid-2026. USDC.n holders must act before March.
data.description: Sei's System Improvement Proposal 3 will phase out support for CosmWasm and transition to exclusively supporting EVM by the middle of 2026. Holders of USDC.n tokens are required to transfer their assets by March to avoid potential loss of access to their funds.

data.title: State Street ends 2025 with $5.7 trillion assets under management, beats earnings estimates with $3.667 billion revenue.
data.description: State Street Corporation (NYSE: STT) experienced a decline in their stock on Friday following the release of their quarterly results and future projections. They reported adjusted earnings per share of $2.97 for the fourth quarter, surpassing the expected $2.85. Quarterly sales reached $3.667 billion, a 7% increase from the previous year and higher than the estimated $3.599 billion. Investment Servicing AUC/A rose by 16% to a new high of $53.8 trillion due to increased market levels and flows. Investment Management AUM also saw growth, rising by 20% to $5.7 trillion. The company introduced 37 new products during the quarter, including 16 ETFs. Fee revenue, a key driver of revenue growth, increased by 8%, with servicing fees up by 8%, management fees by 15%, FX trading by 13%, and securities finance by 8%. Software and processing fees also contributed to this growth.

data.title: Patient Engagement Solutions Market to reach $51.69 billion by 2030, with a growth rate of 12.0% - MarketsandMarkets™.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, as of January 16, 2026, reports from MarketsandMarkets™ indicate significant growth ahead for the Patient Engagement Solutions Market. The market is expected to increase from around USD 29.33 billion recorded in 2025 to a projected USD 51.69 billion by the year 2030, reflecting a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 12.0%. Detailed information can be found by reviewing 577 market data tables and 46 figures related to this market.

data.title: Insurance dispute over destroyed US distribution center: Insurer's claim reduced to 17 mEUR, total exposure 23 mEUR. Nilfisk intends to appeal ruling to Supreme Court.
data.description: Nilfisk is embroiled in a legal battle with its insurer over a payout for the destruction of the company's US distribution center by a tornado. The insurer initially claimed 19 million EUR but this was reduced to 17 million EUR. The Eastern High Court ruled in favor of the insurer, against Nilfisk's expectations. Nilfisk plans to seek leave to appeal the decision to the Supreme Court based on external legal advice. The total exposure for Nilfisk, including interest and legal fees, is approximately 23 million EUR. The CFO expressed disappointment but assured that the dispute is categorized as a special item and will not impact Nilfisk's operating results.

data.title: Krispy Kreme to release limited-edition Championship Dozen on Jan. 19 for football fans with 6 football-shaped doughnuts.
data.description: In Charlotte, N.C., Krispy Kreme® is adding to the anticipation of Monday’s game day for football enthusiasts with the debut of the exclusive Championship Dozen on Jan. 19. To enhance the game-watching experience, fans can now enjoy Krispy Kreme's Championship Dozen, designed for those supporting their teams at home or hosting game-day gatherings with loved ones. The Championship Dozen showcases six football-shaped doughnuts: three coated in orange icing and three in crimson icing.

data.title: Wikimedia Foundation partners with AI giants Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta to provide content for training LLMs.
data.description: The Wikimedia Foundation has announced partnerships with major tech companies Microsoft, Google, Amazon, and Meta to utilize its content for training machine learning models (LLMs).
